Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request For Attorneys Fess And Costs Pursuant To Contract
Off Calendar Jul-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Videoconference via Zoom):
Kelley Harvilla, Esq. 650-458-3339, kharvilla@tongrobbins, of Tong Robbins for Plaintiff.
Jeffrey Loew, Esq. 650-397-8700 of Loew Law Group for Plaintiff.
Peter Weber, Esq. 415-788-1900, Pweber@mpbf.com, of Murphy Pearson Bradley Feeney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT FREELAND, COOPER & FOREMAN LLP, KATE FREELAND, ESQ., DANIEL BERNHARD, ESQ.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request For Attorneys Fess And Costs Pursuant To Contract.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is granted and the court stays this action. Section 8 of the parties' legal services agreements provides for arbitration of this dispute and plaintiffs concede that arbitration is proper. Defendant is entitled to $6,000 as reasonable fees and costs for filing this motion pursuant to section 8 of the agreements. As noted in the motion for sanctions, the court does not conclude that the filing of this complaint was frivolous or that sanctions are warranted. Plaintiffs, however, are contractually obligated to bear the costs that they compelled defendants to incur. The court stays this action rather than dismiss it because the arbitration award will ultimately need to be confirmed, vacated, or corrected.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.5
Off Calendar Jul-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Videoconference via Zoom):
Kelley Harvilla, Esq. 650-458-3339, kharvilla@tongrobbins, of Tong Robbins for Plaintiff.
Jeffrey Loew, Esq. 650-397-8700 of Loew Law Group for Plaintiff.
Peter Weber, Esq. 415-788-1900, Pweber@mpbf.com, of Murphy Pearson Bradley Feeney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT FREELAND, COOPER & FOREMAN LLP, KATE FREELAND, ESQ., DANIEL BERNHARD, ESQ.'s Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.5.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion for sanctions is denied. Defendant fails to demonstrate that plaintiffs filed the instant complaint with subjective bad faith or that the complaint is frivolous. (See In re Marriage of Sahafzadeh-Taeb & Taeb (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 124, 135.) Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. There is no basis to expunge this action from the register of actions and such relief is contrary to the sealing rules of Cal. R. Ct. 2.550 et seq.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
